content.full_text:
84200, et seq.

84200

October 19, 1976

Mr. Samuel Gorlick
City Attorney
P. O. Box 6459
Burbank, California 91510

Dear Mr. Gorlick:

Mr. John Gordnier, of the Attorney General's office,has
referred your letter of September 27 to the Fair Political
Practice Commission for response to your inquiry concerning
filing dates for your February 22, 1977 election.

I will respond to your questions in the order in which they
appear.

1. Candidates and committees should file campaign statements
in connection with the February 22, 1977 election according to the deadlines prescribed in Section 84201 since
there is a possibility of a run-off being held less than
60 days after the primary election. The dates would be:

33 days prior to the February 22 election - January 20, 1977
7 days prior to the election - February 15, 1977
7 days prior to the general election - March 29, 1977
65 days after the April 5 run-off election - June 9, 1977

Those candidates who do not qualify as candidates on the
ballot in the second election and committees supporting
such candidates, are not required to file the campaign
statement due seven days prior to the second election.
They must file their post-election statement no later than
65 days after the general election.

If there is no run-off election, candidates and committees
supporting candidates must file a post-election campaign
statement no later than 65 days after the primary election.

A write-in candidate must file the statement due 7 days
prior to the run-off or general election if he/she qualifies
as a candidate in the run-off or general election. (FPPC
Reg. No. 18421)

Mr. Samuel Gorlick - 2 - October 19, 1976

2. The period covered by candidates and committees who have
never filed a campaign statement is January 7, 1975 as
stated in Section 02046.

3. If an incumbent is seeking re-election and files campaign
statements in conjunction with the February 22, election,
he/she need not file an off-year report for the period
January 1 through June 30 (Section 04206).

Please let me know if I may be of additional assistance.

Very truly yours,

Regina Siciliano-Kutchins
Technical Assistance Program Coordinator

Attachment: FPFC Regulation 10421

CC: John Gordnier

RSK:gh

TO:    Ken Finney
       FFPC
       1100 K St. Bldg.


FROM:  DEPARTMENT OF JUSTICE
       Attorney General
       555 Capitol Mall, Suite 550
       Sacramento, California  95814

CL-12

       CONFIDENTIAL CORRESPONDENCE

CONFIDENTIAL GORLICK, Samuel, 8XX Burbank, Calif.

DATE     10/4          RE: Req. for calendar of filing dates for
                           candidates and committees

COPY TO:

ADMINISTRATION          CONFLICTS           ENFORCEMENT
   BOB F.                  DEB                   BOB S.
   LINDA                   ED                    RICK
   JUDY                    KEN G.               BARBARA
                           PAT                   FRED

EXECUTIVE            LEGAL               TASK
   DEM                   X KEN F.           JOHN K.
   MICKEY                  NATALIE            DAN
   CAROL                    TED             X GINA
                           MIKE                MARYANN
                                           JOHN G.
                                           JAY
                                           RICH


GINA                        Please:

       Take whatever action you consider appropriate
       early directed by    10/15         cc: Jeanne
       [illegible] supported [illegible] and return to_________
       _________________with________________

CITY OF
BURBANK
CALIFORNIA

SAMUEL GORLICK
CITY ATTORNEY

FPPC

Oct 4-4 11 PM '76

RICHARD L. SIEG, JR.
SIDNEY MALECK
DAVID L WILLIAMS
ALAN S KALVIN
ASSISTANTS

MARC L MERRICK
DEPUTY

September 27, 1976

John A. Gordnier, Esq.
Deputy Attorney General
555 Capitol Mall, Suite 550
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear John:

     The City of Burbank will hold elections next year to
fill the offices of Councilman, City Treasurer and City
Clerk. The election dates are February 22, 1977 for the
primary nominating election and April 5, 1977 for the gen-
eral municipal election (to be held only in case a runoff in
any of the offices is necessary).

     I would greatly appreciate a calendar showing the dates
when candidates and their committees must file campaign
statements under the Political Reform Act. I am uncertain
whether to follow Section 84200 or 84201 in setting filing
dates for the primary and whether unsuccessful candidates
and their committees must file their post election statement
under 84200 or 84201 if there is a runoff.

     Another question is the reporting period in the first
campaign statement for candidates and committees that have
never filed a campaign statement before. Section 82046
would seem to indicate that the reporting period for the
first statement commences on January 7, 1975, when the Act
took effect.

     Still another question is whether incumbents who will
be candidates need file a campaign statement under Section
84206.

     There is no immediate urgency for this information but
I must have a calendar prepared by November 26, which is the
first day for filing nomination papers. The City Clerk will
want to hand each candidate a calendar along with campaign

John A. Gordnier, Esq.                          September 27, 1976
Deputy Attorney General                                 Page Two


disclosure forms at the time nomination papers are filed.
We are aware that candidates must file a statement of economic
interests when nomination papers are filed.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [signature]
                                        SAMUEL GORLICK
                                        City Attorney

SG:md

cc:  City Clerk
